free_text: Fission studies of highly excited nuclei
In Russian
. A
part of the data was earlier published in this ref.
Stopping power.
- Correct. method
The targets of Au, W, Ag and Ni were self-supporting.
The targets of Bi and Sb were vacuum evaporated onto
a thin Ni foil, 80 um/cm2, and carbon foil, 40 um/cm2,
respectively. The thicknesses of all targets were
from 200 to 500 um/cm2.
Two-arm detection system with Si-Au
detectors in both arms = variant 1.
Two-arm detection system with Si-Au
detectors in one arm and a position-sensitive
parallel plate avalanche counter in another arm =
variant 2.
Either variant 1 or 2 was used in different
experiments.
Double-grid parallel plate avalanche counter
was used as a start detector in both variants.
Two Si-Au detectors for beam
monitoring with Au target.
Monitor used for the absolute calibration.
Relative monitor.
For the
absolute monitor.
Performed: the pulse height defect in the solid
state detectors was determined according to the
procedure suggested by Kaufman et al.,, see REL-REF.
The energy losses of the fission fragments in the
target, the backing and the start detector were
taken into account using the stopping power data
from Northcliffe et al., see REL-REF.
The random coincidences were eliminated in off-line
analysis.
Angular correlations of fission fragments.
Comparison with results calculated with
Cascade-Evaporation Model.
kinetic energy distribution.
Tables 2,3 from Z.Phys.,A318(1984)97
By Khlopin's Institute - A.P.
Reference, Rel-Ref and some reactions are
corrected by CAJAD.
Rel-Ref, Monit-Ref, Err-Analys, Reactions
And Data Sections are corrected by A.P.
Misc-Err is included for all data sections.
Rel-Ref,Monitor,Monit-Ref and Misc-Col
are corrected by A.P.
Comment with pointers is included in
suitable Subentries by F.C.
Monitor is corrected - A.P.
Date is corrected
Subentries 002,003,004,006 are corrected
according to VML.
Last checking has been done.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. MONIT
reactions updated. ERR-T -> DATA-ERR. MISC-COL deleted,
info presented under REACTION code.
Average total kinetic energy of the fission fragments
prior to prompt neutron emission, obtained by applying
a model-dependent correction.
Quoted errors include inaccuracies in time
and energy calibration procedures, random event
estimation and statistics.
No information about source of uncertainty
